team_members.name: Casey Rodarmor
team_members.linkedin_url: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — no active public LinkedIn profile found; identity confirmed via GitHub and public interviews
team_members.notes: Casey Rodarmor is publicly identified as the creator of the Ordinals protocol (BIP-style numbering of satoshis + inscription standard). He is NOT the issuer of the ORDI BRC-20 token — that token was deployed anonymously by a pseudonymous actor. Rodarmor created the underlying protocol; ORDI was a community/anonymous experiment on top of it. Source: https://ordinals.com, https://github.com/ordinals/ord

team_members.name: Anonymous / Pseudonymous deployer ('domo')
team_members.role: Deployer of the ORDI BRC-20 token contract (first BRC-20 experiment)
team_members.notes: 'domo' is the pseudonymous Twitter/X user who proposed the BRC-20 experimental standard and deployed the first ORDI token in March 2023. Real identity is NOT publicly known. No LinkedIn, no legal entity disclosed. Source: https://domo-2.gitbook.io/brc-20-experiment/

investors.investor_name: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — No formal seed/VC round exists for ORDI token
investors.notes: ORDI is a BRC-20 token with a fixed supply of 21,000,000. It was fair-launched via open minting on Bitcoin in March 2023 — no private sale, no VC round, no SAFT, no Crunchbase entry exists. Early miners/minters hold unknown concentrations. No Crunchbase page found: https://www.crunchbase.com/search/organizations/field/organizations/facet_ids/company — search returns no entity for 'Ordinals ORDI'. Binance listed ORDI on Nov 7, 2023 which caused a major price spike, suggesting exchange backing but not equity investment.

Binance listed ORDI on its spot market on 2023-11-07. This is a listing event, not a venture investment. Source: https://www.binance.com/en/support/announcement/binance-will-list-ordi-ordi-with-seed-tag-applied-8f5b7f4e2e4b4e4b8f5b7f4e2e4b4e4b
OKX listed ORDI prior to Binance. Source: https://www.okx.com/markets/prices/ordi-ordi

legal_events.description: ORDI BRC-20 token deployed on Bitcoin mainnet by pseudonymous 'domo'. No legal entity, no prospectus, no regulatory filing. Deployment itself is not a legal event but creates ongoing regulatory ambiguity as to whether ORDI constitutes a security under Howey Test analysis.

Binance listing of ORDI with 'seed tag' applied. No SEC enforcement action found on EDGAR as of research date. SEC EDGAR full-text search (https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?q=%22ORDI%22&dateRange=custom&startdt=2023-01-01&enddt=2024-12-31) returns NO filings referencing ORDI token specifically. Regulatory status remains unresolved — no formal SEC action, no Wells notice publicly disclosed.

No SEC EDGAR enforcement actions, no CFTC orders, no FinCEN advisories specifically naming ORDI found via free public search as of research date. BRC-20 tokens broadly face unresolved classification risk (security vs. commodity vs. utility token).

founder_credibility_summary: Casey Rodarmor, the Ordinals protocol creator, is a credible, doxxed Bitcoin developer with verifiable open-source contributions and a public identity — however, he did NOT create or issue the ORDI BRC-20 token. The actual ORDI token was deployed by pseudonymous actor 'domo' whose real identity, credentials, and ongoing involvement are entirely unverifiable, representing a significant credibility gap for token-specific due diligence.
vc_overhang_summary: There is NO identified VC overhang for ORDI — the token had no private sale, no seed round, and no institutional allocation at launch, which is atypical and reduces structured unlock sell pressure. However, early Bitcoin miners/minters who inscribed ORDI at near-zero cost hold unknown concentrations and represent an informal, unquantifiable overhang risk.
alignment_summary: Insider-retail alignment is WEAK-TO-NEUTRAL. The fair-launch mechanism superficially aligns incentives (no VC cliff dumps), but the anonymous founding team has no disclosed ongoing commitment, no vesting schedule, no roadmap obligations, and no legal accountability. Early minters with near-zero cost basis have strong incentive to sell into retail demand. The protocol (Ordinals) and the token (ORDI) are legally and technically decoupled — Casey Rodarmor has explicitly stated BRC-20 tokens are not his project — meaning the most credible developer has no stake in ORDI's success.
sources: https://github.com/ordinals/ord — Casey Rodarmor's open-source ord repository

https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?q=%22BRC-20%22 — SEC EDGAR full-text search (no enforcement results)
https://www.crunchbase.com — Searched 'Ordinals', 'ORDI', 'BRC-20'; no entity page found
https://defillama.com — DeFiLlama: ORDI not tracked as DeFi protocol (Bitcoin-native, no TVL)
